mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a three-month window signals an impending recession, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in housing starts and industrial production indicates a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1100 AND INDPRO falls below 105
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive when both housing and industrial indicators breach their thresholds simultaneously, or the joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Stagflationary conditions where real GDP growth decelerates while consumer price inflation accelerates erode corporate profit margins and force central bank policy paralysis, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets deliver positive quarterly returns during confirmed stagflation episodes, or the combined GDPC1-CPIAUCSL spread shows no predictive power for forward returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly employment growth directly validates corporate revenue assumptions and supports near-term earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no statistically significant correlation with monthly payroll changes, or the signal generates negative returns in more than 55 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds consumer price inflation by more than 1.5 percentage points, real borrowing costs become restrictive, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL growth rate exceeds 1.5 percent
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for 20 days following the crossing of the real rate threshold, or the interaction term fails to outperform a single FEDFUNDS signal in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in revolving consumer credit and housing market activity reflects robust household balance sheet health, fueling consumption-driven earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly forward returns turn negative when both credit and housing indicators rise concurrently, or the joint signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production breaching a 110 index level signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns remain flat or negative when industrial production sustains levels above 110, or the threshold signal produces a false positive rate exceeding 70 percent in backtesting.
